mechanism_narrative: A sustained low federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no significant positive mean during low-rate periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below five percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, which directly boosts corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 55% of instances where the trigger condition is met, or the signal loses predictive power when inflation exceeds 4%.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated household savings rates combined with rising consumer prices indicate pent-up demand that outpaces inflation, increasing corporate revenue growth and producing positive equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: The interaction term between savings and inflation loses statistical significance with a p-value above 0.10 in multivariate regression over 2-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rapid industrial production growth exceeding two percent quarter-over-quarter resolves supply chain bottlenecks and stabilizes input costs, supporting profit margins and generating positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses to negative when GDP growth exceeds 4% annually, or 5-day forward returns show negative skew during high production growth periods.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation while policy rates remain anchored near zero forces real interest rates deeply negative, compelling institutional investors to rebalance portfolios into equities and generating positive five-day returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no significant positive skew with a Jarque-Bera p-value above 0.05 during triggered periods, or the signal fails when VIX exceeds 25.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts alongside falling household savings signals weakening consumer balance sheets and reduced demand for durable goods, dragging down corporate earnings and causing negative equity returns.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns remain positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint decline in housing and savings fails to predict earnings downgrades.

mechanism_narrative: Stagnant nonfarm payrolls growth combined with a rising unemployment rate indicates labor market deterioration that reduces aggregate demand and corporate earnings expectations, causing negative equity returns over one month.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window despite the negative signal, or forward returns are positive in greater than 50% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong quarterly GDP growth reflects robust economic activity that translates into higher corporate earnings and cash flows, producing positive equity returns over a one-month horizon regardless of the business cycle phase.
failure_condition: Forward returns are negative in greater than 50% of observations when GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% quarter-over-quarter, or the correlation coefficient drops below 0.10.
